one_line_definition: Quasi-static model treating a thin fluid layer as a spring of stiffness K=ρc²/h, relating ultrasonic reflection coefficient to layer thickness at sub-resonance frequencies
Ratio of reflected to incident ultrasonic amplitude at an interface or layer; depends on acoustic impedances and frequency
Product of material density and speed of sound (z=ρc); governs reflection and transmission at interfaces
Frequency at which the film thickness equals half a wavelength (h=c/2f); at this frequency the layer is acoustically transparent
Lubrication regime in highly-loaded non-conformal contacts where elastic deformation and pressure-dependent viscosity both play roles
EHL regime where lubricant viscosity remains approximately constant (typical of polymer/rubber contacts); large elastic deformation, low contact pressure
Film-thickness technique using the phase change of the reflected ultrasonic pulse, valid for acoustically dissimilar contacts where the amplitude method fails
Path length in a viscous liquid over which shear-wave amplitude falls to 1/e; δ=(2η/ωρ)^(1/2); determines minimum sample volume for viscometry
Ratio of stress to particle velocity at the surface of a probe immersed in a medium; encapsulates all effects of the surrounding material on probe damping
Transducer using a magnetostrictive alloy strip that converts magnetic excitation into mechanical shear vibrations; basis of the Ultra-Viscoson viscometer
Elastic wave that propagates along a solid surface with displacement decaying exponentially into the bulk; sensitive to surface contacts and coatings
Thickness measurement from the time between two discrete ultrasonic echoes; applicable only when film thickness > ~3 pulse wavelengths (~40 µm)
Wave propagation model enforcing continuity of stress and displacement at each layer boundary; valid for all film thicknesses and provides resonance predictions
Equation of state relating lubricant bulk modulus and density to applied pressure; used to estimate speed of sound in lubricant under EHD conditions
Empirical regression equation for central EHD film thickness as a function of load, speed, lubricant viscosity, pressure-viscosity coefficient, and contact geometry
Viscosity measured by oscillatory (ultrasonic) methods; may differ from static (zero-frequency) flow viscosity for non-Newtonian materials
Fluid whose viscosity depends on shear rate or frequency; requires specification of measurement conditions for meaningful results
The reflection coefficient from a thin layer is a function of frequency × thickness, not of either independently; allows prediction of R at unmeasured thicknesses
Transducer with a curved element or lens that concentrates ultrasonic energy at a focal point, improving spatial resolution in film measurements
Transducer with a wedge-shaped coupling element used to excite surface or shear waves at a specific angle into a solid component
Formation of vapour or gas bubbles in the low-pressure exit zone of an EHL contact; detected ultrasonically as signal anomalies in the exit region
Frequency halfway between two consecutive layer resonances; at this frequency R_half = (z1·z3 − z2²)/(z1·z3 + z2²), maximum sensitivity to layer impedance
